headline: H1 ends with a divergence: Dow hits record, Nasdaq bleeds on AI fatigue, gold reclaims $4,000.

news.headline: Equal-weight S&P rises as Dow posts record close; tech complex staggers
news.context: Classic end-of-quarter rotation: defensives + value bid, mega-cap tech under pressure into H2.

news.headline: Gold reclaims $4,000 after cooler inflation print caps rate-hike bets
news.context: Real yields ease, dollar softens — first close above $4k since the prior week's hawkish-Fed fade.

news.headline: NVDA slides as AI-complex unwind deepens; semis lead Nasdaq down
news.context: Beta unwind plus AI capex scrutiny = vol-friendly tape. Watch $195 as next NVDA support.

news.headline: WTI holds $71.50 on Oman vessel incident; OPEC+ chatter secondary
news.context: Geopolitical premium sticky — E&P names carried energy tape as only green sector.

news.headline: BTC defends $60k but ETF flows stay muted into the long weekend
news.context: Crypto traders watching $60k pivot; no bid from spot ETFs Friday, futures basis compressing.

options.thesis: $124M delta-hedged QQQ block crossed print — desks harvesting vol into quarter-end rebalance.
options.signal: Premium-selling day: $280M notional sold, watch dealer gamma flip into July open.

options.thesis: VIX +12% on the day while SPY IV lagged — dispersion trade ripe into July CPI.
options.signal: Long VIX / long SPY put spread, target July 11 expiry.

options.thesis: Unusual call sweeps on July $260 strikes; delivery-narrative trade re-emerging.
options.signal: Block trade detected, retail following — fade if no delivery confirmation by Tue.

options.thesis: IV crushed post-selloff (rank ~22) while realized vol stayed elevated — vol gift into Q2 print.
options.signal: Long July straddle, $190/$210 strikes; cheap relative to 30-day realized.

options.thesis: Energy the only green sector — XLE call flow quietly bid $95/$97 July strikes.
options.signal: Surprise long: oil-beta reflation trade with tight $3 risk into Iran/OPEC headlines.

takeaway: H1 closes with a clear regime split: defensives/Dow leadership, AI complex under repair, gold and oil bid on macro stress. July opens with CPI + jobs tape — vol is cheap, flow is short-premium. Size up selectively, lean into dispersion, fade undiscriminating AI-bear calls.

headline: Risk-off into the close: Apple drops 6% on OpenAI IPO delay, semis drag Nasdaq, oil bid on Oman incident.

news.headline: Apple drops 6% as OpenAI IPO delay triggers AI complex unwind
news.context: AAPL is the largest SPX weight by market cap — a 6% drag alone explains the Nasdaq's 1.5% slide.

news.headline: Post-FOMC rhetoric tilts hawkish — MNI flags fresh hike framing
news.context: Sticks to the 'higher for longer' script; front-end repricing risk into July CPI.

news.headline: Nvidia slide signals S&P 500 risks as AI chip run slows
news.context: Semis lead the beta unwind; NVDA weakness = broader AI complex pressure into next week.

news.headline: WTI holds gains near $71.50 after cargo vessel incident near Oman
news.context: Geopolitical risk premium back in the barrel — energy the only green sector on the tape.

news.headline: Gold slips under $4,000 — fourth weekly loss on hawkish Fed, strong dollar
news.context: Haven demand fading as real yields push higher; watch $3,950 as next support.

options.thesis: $124M delta-hedged QQQ block crossed print — desks harvesting vol on semis/software.
options.signal: Premium-selling day: $280M notional sold, skew flattens, watch for dealer gamma flip.

options.thesis: VIX up 12% on the day with SPY IV lagging — dispersion trade set up.
options.signal: Long VIX / long SPY put spread into next week's jobs print.

options.thesis: Unusual call flow re-emerges on delivery chatter; July $260 strikes bid.
options.signal: Sweep activity into Friday close — narrative-trade, not flow-confirmed yet.

options.thesis: IV crushed into AI-complex selloff — vol is cheap relative to realized.
options.signal: Long straddle into next print; vol is being gifted here.

takeaway: Tape ends H1 on a sour note — AAPL-led AI unwind + hawkish Fed reframe = defensive rotation into the long weekend. Size down, watch $7,300 ES as Friday closing support.

headline: Futures flat into long-weekend tape, gold bid, BTC heavy.

news.headline: Fed minutes signal 'patient' stance; markets price 60% Sept hold
news.context: Dot-plot chatter settles back to data-dependent. Keeps duration bid early.

news.headline: NVDA down 2% pre-market after Asia supplier warning
news.context: Supply chain comment, not a guide cut — but the AI complex trades the headline.

news.headline: Oil slides as OPEC+ flags earlier-than-expected unwind
news.context: Energy drag on indices; E&P names trade softer into the bell.

news.headline: BTC reclaims $62k but ETF flows stay muted
news.context: Crypto traders watching $60k as the line in the sand for the weekend.

options.thesis: Put/call ratio at 0.62 with weekly expiry pinned at $540 — dealers short gamma into Friday close.
options.signal: Gamma squeeze risk if ES tags 5,450 into lunch.

options.thesis: Front-week IV at 11 vol — cheap for any post-CPI gap. Look at 480/490 strangles into next print.
Unusual call sweep on $260 Jul-5 expiry, ~$4.2M notional. Targeting delivery numbers next week.
options.signal: Block trade on the tape, retail following.

options.thesis: Meme rotation probe — IV up 30% in 48h but OTM call flow thin. Avoid chasing, watch $25 as pivot.

takeaway: Tape wants to chop higher into the long weekend. Stay nimble on Friday — gamma pin plus low volume is a setup for an end-of-day whipsaw.

headline: Futures green on cooling CPI, rates rally, beta bid.

news.headline: Core CPI prints 3.1% YoY, in-line; services decelerates
news.context: Bond market likes the drift. Front-end futures now price 1 cut by year-end.

news.headline: Mega-cap tech leads, semis up 1.8% pre-market
news.context: Risk-on rotation. Small caps lagging into the open.

options.thesis: Call flow heavy on the 490/500 strikes for Jul expiry.
options.signal: Dealers long gamma — supportive of trend days.

options.thesis: IV compressed into CPI; next move likely 3–4% on follow-through.

takeaway: Soft CPI is the green light. Risk-on into close, but watch the Fed speakers at 2pm for any pushback.
